nodes.x: Verily thus saith the Lord unto you, my servant Lyman: Your sins are forgiven you, because you have obeyed my voice in coming up hither this morning to receive counsel of him whom I have appointed.
And behold, verily I say unto you, blessed are you who are now hearing these words of mine from the mouth of my servant, for your sins are forgiven you.
Behold, thus saith the Lord unto my people—you have many things to do and to repent of; for behold, your sins have come up unto me, and are not pardoned, because you seek to counsel in your own ways.
Behold, thy sins are forgiven thee, and thou art an elect lady, whom I have called.
Thus saith the Lord, verily, verily I say unto you my son, thy sins are forgiven thee, according to thy petition, for thy prayers and the prayers of thy brethren have come up into my ears.
And now concerning my servant Parley P. Pratt, behold, I say unto him that as I live I will that he shall declare my gospel and learn of me, and be meek and lowly of heart.
Therefore, let your soul be at rest concerning your spiritual standing, and resist no more my voice.
And it shall be given unto you, power over that spirit; and you shall proclaim against that spirit with a loud voice that it is not of God—
Wherefore, lift up your voice and spare not, for the Lord God hath spoken; therefore prophesy, and it shall be given by the power of the Holy Ghost.

1 It is with feelings of pleasuro and satisfaction that I embrace the privilege of standing before you. I request your careful attention, together with your faith and prayers while I talk for a short time. I do not know what I shall say, neither shall I until it is delivered.
Awake, my soul! No longer droop in sin. Rejoice, O my heart, and give place no more for the enemy of my soul.
And arise up and be more careful henceforth in observing your vows, which you have made and do make, and you shall be blessed with exceeding great blessings.
Thy vows are upon me, O God: I will render praises unto thee.
Nevertheless thy vows shall be offered up in righteousness on all days and at all times;
I will pay my vows unto the Lord now in the presence of all his people,
I will pay my vows unto the Lord now in the presence of all his people.
My praise shall be of thee in the great congregation: I will pay my vows before them that fear him.
Wait patiently until the solemn assembly shall be called of my servants, then you shall be remembered with the first of mine elders, and receive right by ordination with the rest of mine elders whom…
Behold, this commandment is given unto all the elders whom I have chosen.
Call your solemn assemblies, and speak often one to another. And let every man call upon the name of the Lord.

And now concerning the gathering, let the bishop and the agent make preparations for those families which have been commanded to come to this land, as soon as possible, and plant them in their inheritance. And unto the residue of both elders and members, further directions shall be given hereafter; even so: Amen.
Behold, this is the promise of the Father unto you if you continue faithful.
Therefore, be faithful; and behold, and lo, I am with you even unto the end. Even so. Amen.
For he will fulfil all his promises which he shall make unto you, for he has fulfilled his promises which he has made unto our fathers.
And it shall come to pass, if they are not more faithful unto me, it shall be taken away, even that which they have.
This promise is yours also, because ye are of Abraham, and the promise was made unto Abraham; and by this law is the continuation of the works of my Father, wherein he glorifieth himself.
This much did the Father command me, that I should tell unto them:
And it shall be fulfilled upon you in that day that you shall have right to preach my gospel wheresoever I shall send you, from henceforth from that time.
And it shall come to pass that the time cometh, when the fulness of my gospel shall be preached unto them;

And thus commandeth the Father that I should say unto you: At that day when the Gentiles shall sin against my gospel, and shall reject the fulness of my gospel, and shall be lifted up in the pride of their hearts above all nations, and above all the people of the whole earth, and shall be filled with all manner of lyings, and of deceits, and of mischiefs, and all manner of hypocrisy, and murders,…

And they shall be a scourge unto the people of this land. Nevertheless, when they shall have received the fulness of my gospel, then if they shall harden their hearts against me I will return their iniquities upon their own heads, saith the Father.
And he shall send Jesus Christ, which before was preached unto you:
Verily I say unto you, Wheresoever this gospel shall be preached in the whole world, there shall also this, that this woman hath done, be told for a memorial of her.
Therefore, strengthen your brethren in all your conversation, in all your prayers, in all your exhortations, and in all your doings.

Beloved Brethren and Friends—Since it has fallen to my lot to address you this afternoon, I hope you will not only lend me your undivided attention, but favor me with an interest in your prayers, that I may ever speak according to the mind and will of God, upon all those subjects which may engage my attention. I shall take a text, according to the mode and fashion of the day; yet, I

In arising to address you, my brethren and sisters, this evening, I desire an interest in your faith and prayers, that I may be led to speak upon those points of doctrine or of principles, that are adapted to our
And behold, and lo, I am with you to bless you and deliver you forever. Amen.
again in your midst. I ask the Lord to bless us, one and all, with his Holy Spirit, and to guide us in the way of life. Amen.
